content:
I Thought I Was Selling AI Automation. What Businesses Actually Wanted Was a Better Way to Think About Prompts.

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Mariia Shalabaieva on UnsplashWhen ChatGPT first exploded in popularity, I did what most developers did.
I started building AI tools.
Chatbots.
Content generators.
Customer support assistants.
Workflow automations.
The technology was impressive.
The results were inconsistent.
Sometimes the AI produced incredible output.
Other times it generated something that looked like it was written by a sleep-deprived intern at 3 AM.
The issue wasn’t the model.
It was the prompt.
After dozens of client projects, I noticed something interesting:
The companies getting the best results weren’t using better AI.
They were using better prompting systems.
So I began developing reusable prompt frameworks instead of individual prompts.
